title: Packaging the tool to share
question: How should the project be packaged so other people can install and use the decision-tree tool?
rationale: Ship the whole tool as real copies in a 'skill/' folder (SKILL.md, generate.py, the viewer, the consult/audit flows, and the backstop hook) plus a README with install and usage steps. Chosen so anyone can clone the repo and get a working tool, without it disturbing the one-shared-viewer setup (d35) you use day to day. Trade-off: 'skill/' is a copy of your live install, so you re-copy it when the viewer changes.

options.label: Real copies in a skill/ folder + README

tradeoffs.note: others get the full tool with no dangling symlinks and no dependency on your machine
clone, copy skill/ into ~/.claude/skills, and /decision-tree works on their own projects
skill/ is a copy of your live install, so you re-copy the viewer/SKILL when you change them (the d35 shared-viewer trade)
your own projects keep using the single symlinked viewer (d35) — the package sits beside it, nothing rewired

options.label: Leave it as symlinks only (the old way)

tradeoffs.note: a fresh clone has broken viewer links and no skill — it only works on your machine
nothing to re-copy; the one shared viewer is the only copy

options.label: Restructure the repo to be the skill itself (files at the root)

tradeoffs.note: the repo is the tool, nothing nested
would move your decisions/ and rewire the working project for little gain over a skill/ folder
